# ccc — call coding CLIs

> One command to run any coding CLI. A unified interface implemented in 20 programming languages.

ccc is a command-line tool that wraps terminal-first coding agents (OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, Cursor Agent, Gemini CLI, Crush) behind a single consistent interface. Each of the 20 language implementations bundles an identical `ccc` CLI binary and a reusable library with `CommandSpec`, `CompletedRun`, and `Runner` types.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc --print-config`, which prints the canonical example config with the current supported keys, and `ccc config`, which prints every existing resolved config file path and contents.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc config --edit [--user|--local]`, which opens the selected user or project-local config in `$EDITOR`.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc add [-g] <alias>`, a line-prompt alias wizard that writes `[aliases.<name>]` entries to config; scriptable flags plus `--yes` can write aliases non-interactively.
Python and Rust also write a per-run artifact directory under the platform state root, with a client-prefixed run folder such as `opencode-<run-id>`, `output.txt`, exactly one transcript file, and a stable stderr footer line that scripts can parse as `>> ccc:output-log >> /abs/path/to/run-dir`; `--output-log-path` enables the footer and `--no-output-log-path` suppresses it.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc -v` / `ccc --version`, which prints the repo build version plus resolved client versions; the build version is sourced from the repo-root `VERSION` file at build time.

## Usage

ccc [controls...] "<Prompt>"

Slots are parsed in free order before the prompt, except that `help`, `-h`, and `--help` win anywhere in argv and print help immediately:
- runner: Select which coding CLI (default: oc). Options: opencode (oc), claude (cc), kimi (k), cursor (cu), gemini (g), codex (c/cx), roocode (rc), crush (cr)
- +thinking: Set thinking level from +0 (off) to +4 (max)
  Claude keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--thinking disabled`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking enabled` with matching `--effort`.
  Kimi keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--no-thinking`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking`.
- --show-thinking: Request visible thinking output when supported; default on. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --save-session: Explicitly allow the selected runner's normal session persistence. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --cleanup-session: Try post-run cleanup when the selected runner lacks a no-persist flag. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --output-log-path / --no-output-log-path: emit or suppress the final run-directory footer for scripts
- --timeout-secs <N>: Kill the wrapped runner after N seconds; Python and Rust exit 124 and print `warning: timed out after N seconds; killed runner` to stderr
- --output-mode: Choose `text`, `stream-text`, `json`, `stream-json`, `formatted`, or `stream-formatted`
  Dot-sugar aliases are available before the prompt: `.text` / `..text`, `.json` / `..json`, `.fmt` / `..fmt`.
  Human-formatted modes honor `FORCE_COLOR` and `NO_COLOR` before falling back to TTY detection.
  Formatted run transcripts always retain unhandled structured JSON lines; `CCC_FWD_UNKNOWN_JSON` controls stderr forwarding and currently defaults on.
- :provider:model: Override provider and model
- @name: Use a named preset from the loaded config layers; when no preset exists, runner names such as `@k` select that runner before ordinary agent fallback; in Python and Rust, presets can also provide a default prompt when the user leaves prompt text blank, and alias `prompt_mode = "prepend"|"append"` can wrap explicitly supplied prompt text with alias prompt text
- Config: in Python and Rust, project-local `.ccc.toml` files are searched upward from the current directory, then `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ccc/config.toml`, then `~/.config/ccc/config.toml`
- Generated config: `ccc --print-config` prints the canonical schema using `[defaults]`, `[abbreviations]`, and `[aliases.<name>]`
- Resolved config: `ccc config` prints all active config file paths and their raw contents in merge order; `CCC_CONFIG` wins alone when it points at an existing file
- Config editing: `ccc config --edit` opens the selected config in `$EDITOR`; `--user` targets the XDG/home user config and `--local` targets the nearest `.ccc.toml`, creating one in the current directory if needed
- Alias wizard: `ccc add <alias>` writes the active write target; `-g` forces the effective global config instead of project-local config; blank/default answers omit alias keys; menu prompt color follows TTY detection and honors FORCE_COLOR/NO_COLOR; the wizard skips `prompt_mode` when prompt is unset and final save confirmation only accepts `y`, `n`, `yes`, or `no`
- `ccc` writes `transcript.txt` for text and human transcript paths, `transcript.jsonl` for JSON-oriented paths, keeps `text` requests upgraded into structured streaming on `transcript.txt`, and places the files under a client-prefixed run directory such as `opencode-<run-id>`

Session persistence:
- Python and Rust add Claude `--no-session-persistence` and Codex `--ephemeral` by default.
- OpenCode, Kimi, Cursor, Gemini, Crush, and RooCode may still save sessions by default; Python and Rust warn unless `--save-session` or `--cleanup-session` is used.
- `--cleanup-session` currently tries safe cleanup for OpenCode and Kimi only. Cursor, Gemini, Crush, and RooCode warn because cleanup would require guessing.

Examples:
- ccc "Fix the failing tests"
- ccc --show-thinking "Fix the failing tests"
- ccc ..fmt cc +3 "Investigate the failing test"
- ccc .json oc "Reply with exactly ok"
- ccc cc +2 :anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514 @reviewer "Add tests"
- ccc c +4 :openai:gpt-5.4-mini @agent "Debug the parser"
- ccc k +4 "Debug the parser"
- ccc cu .fmt "Debug the parser"
- ccc --print-config
- ccc config
- EDITOR=vim ccc config --edit --local
- ccc add mm27 --runner cc --prompt "Review changes" --prompt-mode default --yes

## Languages

Implemented in: Python, Rust, TypeScript, C, Go, Ruby, Perl, C++, Zig, D, F#, Haskell, Nim, Crystal, PHP, PureScript, Elixir, OCaml, VBScript, x86-64 ASM.

All implementations share: build_prompt_spec, CommandSpec, CompletedRun, Runner.run, ccc CLI, prompt trimming, empty prompt rejection, usage on bad args, exit code forwarding, and startup failure reporting.

Python and Rust also support real-runner override env vars for local smoke/debugging:
- `CCC_REAL_OPENCODE`
- `CCC_REAL_CLAUDE`
- `CCC_REAL_KIMI`
- `CCC_REAL_CURSOR`
- `CCC_REAL_GEMINI`

18 of 20 languages also implement: v2 parser/config (runner selector, thinking levels, provider/model override, preset and agent resolution, config loading) and v3 JSON output parsing (OpenCode, Claude Code, Kimi schemas). Python and Rust also parse Cursor Agent and Gemini JSON output, including formatted Gemini transcript modes.

## Docs

- [CLI Reference](https://xertrov.github.io/call-coding-clis/): Full usage, slots, examples, feature matrix, build and test instructions
- [JSON Event References](clis/json-event-references.md): structured-output references for Claude, Kimi, OpenCode, and Cursor Agent
- [Mock Smoke Recipes](clis/mock-smoke.md): mock-runner smoke commands for formatted and raw output checks

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/xertrov/call-coding-clis): Source code, all 20 language implementations
